name: Benny Davis
description: American vaudeville performer and writer of popular songs (1895–1979)
abstract: Benny Davis (August 21, 1895 - December 20, 1979) was a vaudeville performer and writer of popular songs.

works_created.t: Give Me a Song to Sing

about_quotes.speaker: Dominic James "Nick" La Rocca
about_quotes.evidence: I asked him about what he was going to do to three of them. That was Conrad and Benny Davis, and he, what he gave us, a thousand dollars for plugging his tune.

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Lopez
about_quotes.evidence: And we had some entertainers on there. Benny Fields, Benny Davis, Jack Salisbury, Charlie Thorpe. And I had the band.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: Ben Davis was the composer of a lot of music, Benny Davis. He wrote a lot of music for the old Cotton Club.

about_quotes.speaker: George "Jug" Jernberg
about_quotes.evidence: And Benny Fields and Benny Davis and Jack Salisbury were a singing trio in the Windcliff Inn.
